data.title: Suncor oil worker death company's first since 2022 CEO departure
data.description:
Provincial authorities in Alberta said Thursday they are investigating last week's death of a worker at Suncor Energy's (SU) Fort Hills oil sands site near Fort McMurray, the company's first on-site fatality since a former CEO stepped down in 2022 following a series of deadly incidents.

Suncor (SU) said the worker was operating a piece of heavy equipment on January 13 when it sank into the muskeg, a type of watery bog; the person has still not been found, and the company said the incident may have resulted from a medical event.

The fatality marks the first at a Suncor (SU) site since 2022, when a spate of incidents prompted calls for a major shakeup of the company, with activist Elliott Investment Management leading the campaign that resulted in then-CEO Mark Little stepping down; he was replaced by former Imperial Oil head Rich Kruger, who remains in charge at Suncor.

data.title: Old Republic signals continued specialty growth and pricing discipline into 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Old Republic International Corporation (ORI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Craig Smiddy, President, CEO & Director, stated that "in the fourth quarter, we produced $236 million of consolidated pretax operating income compared to $285 million, and our consolidated combined ratio was 96% compared to 92.7%." He highlighted a full year pretax operating income of $1 billion and a consolidated combined ratio of 94.7%. Smiddy pointed out growth in book value per share, including dividends, of 22%, attributing it to "strong operating earnings, our higher investment valuations and our sound capital management strategy."

  • Specialty Insurance grew net premiums earned by 8.3% in the quarter and 10.9% for the year, exceeding $5 billion for the first time. Specialty produced $178 million of pretax operating income for the quarter and $900 million for the year, with a combined ratio for the year at 93.2%. Title grew premium and fees by 12.4% in the quarter and 9.1% for the year, with $65 million of pretax operating income in Q4 and a combined ratio of 94%.

  • Francis Sodaro, Senior VP & CFO, stated, "This morning, we reported net operating income of $185 million for the quarter compared to $227 million last year. On a per share basis, comparable quarter-over-quarter results were $0.74 compared to $0.90." He noted a 7.9% increase in net investment income for the quarter and capital return initiatives of over $1 billion for the year, with $850 million remaining in the repurchase program.

  • Carolyn Monroe, President, reported, "Title reported premium and fee revenue for the quarter of $789 million. This represents an increase of 12% from fourth quarter of last year." She emphasized commercial premiums rising to 29% of earned premiums, investment income up nearly 12%, and expense management resulting in a 1.2% decrease in operating expenses relative to premium and fees.

Outlook

  • Smiddy described plans for 2026: "ending the year at a combined ratio in Specialty of 93.2% feels pretty good to us. We're hopefully, over time, be a bit better than that. But I think where we're at in the P&C cycle, that feels pretty good. And relative to next year, our plan is to produce something around the same level." He added, "I would say 2026, we're looking for a very consistent year."

  • Monroe noted, "commercial should have about a 15% to 20% improvement over this year, which was a wonderful year for commercial. But they see a single-digit increase in residential for next year, less than 10%. So... another year like this year, with some improvement, especially if commercial does improve like it did."

Financial Results

  • Net operating income for the quarter was $185 million, with $0.74 per share. Net investment income rose 7.9% for the quarter. Book value per share ended at $24.21, marking a 22% increase for the year. Nearly $700 million in dividends were declared, and $56 million in share repurchases occurred during the quarter. Total capital return for the year exceeded $1 billion.

  • Specialty Insurance net premiums written increased 6.1% in the quarter. New Specialty operating companies contributed over $300 million in net premiums written in 2025 and delivered positive operating income.

  • Commercial auto net premiums written grew 6.4% in the quarter, with the loss ratio at 80%. Rate increases accelerated to 16% for commercial auto. Workers' comp net premiums written was 6% lower, with a loss ratio of 65.2%. Property net premiums written increased 21% in the quarter.

Q&A

  • Charles Peters, Raymond James: Asked about Specialty Insurance combined ratio targets for 2026 and drivers of higher loss picks. Craig Smiddy responded, "ending the year at a combined ratio in Specialty of 93.2% feels pretty good to us... our plan is to produce something around the same level." On loss picks, Smiddy explained, "as we got towards the end of the year, while we did not notice any paid claim difference, we did notice that case reserves were higher... trends now look to be rather than in the low teens, the mid-teens."

  • Peters: Inquired about capital position post-dividend. Smiddy replied, "we still think we have plenty of capital. So we think that will put us in a position to, again, recommend to the Board a regular dividend increase... we also have $850 million still remaining on our share repurchase program."

  • Jon Paul Newsome, Piper Sandler: Asked about patterns in case reserves and commercial auto trends. Smiddy explained, "the number of people making bodily injury claims... is higher. And the percentage of bodily injury claims with attorney representation is higher... our view is that litigation system abuse is accounting for these increases."

  • Newsome: Questioned risk management in commercial auto. Smiddy noted, "our most sophisticated data and analytics area is within our long-haul trucking company... helps us with targeting rate to those customers that elevate higher propensity for loss."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was inquisitive but mildly cautious, with repeated questions about reserving practices, loss trends, and capital management.

  • Management's tone was confident and steady in prepared remarks, but more defensive and detailed in Q&A, especially in discussing loss trends and reserving, as seen in Smiddy's comments: "it's impossible to instantaneously know what your severity is... the best anyone can do is make a projection based on current observations."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst focus remained on capital deployment and reserving. Management's confidence was consistent but more explicit about rapid response to loss trend changes.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to a focus on maintaining combined ratios at current levels, with less emphasis on expansion. Strategic focus highlighted ongoing investment in Specialty operating companies and technology.

  • Analysts continued to focus on capital management and reserving discipline. Key metric changes included higher rate increases in commercial auto and a sharper rise in property premium growth.

  • Management's confidence remained steady, but there was increased discussion of rising loss trends, especially in commercial auto, and a stronger emphasis on rate discipline and risk selection.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited higher case reserves, increased bodily injury claims, greater attorney involvement, and litigation system abuse as ongoing risks in commercial auto.

  • Smiddy explained, "trends now look to be rather than in the low teens, the mid-teens... litigation system abuse is accounting for these increases."

  • Analysts highlighted concerns about reserving adequacy and the impact of capital returns on future flexibility.

Final Takeaway

Old Republic closed 2025 with record operating income and continued robust capital returns, driven by growth in Specialty and Title segments and supported by disciplined underwriting and rate management. Management aims to maintain profitability and combined ratios into 2026, while closely monitoring loss trends—particularly in commercial auto—and maintaining flexibility for capital deployment through dividends and share repurchases. The company remains focused on underwriting discipline and investment in technology and new Specialty companies to support long-term growth and resilience in a shifting market environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Shell considers exit from Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale play - Reuters
data.description:
Shell (SHEL) is considering a sale of its assets in Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale play and has approached potential buyers in recent weeks to gauge their interest, Reuters reported Thursday.

Shell (SHEL) is said to be open to selling some or all of its interests in the Vaca Muerta, and the company's assets likely would be valued in the billions of dollars, although a precise estimate is difficult because some of the assets are undeveloped, the report said.

The company entered Vaca Muerta in 2012, and a full sale would mark a surprise exit by one of the play's earliest backers, just as interest in the region is growing due to concerns that other large shale fields have peaked.

Shale pioneer Harold Hamm's Continental Resources recently acquired minority stakes in four Vaca Muerta blocks from Pan American Energy, calling the region "one of the most compelling shale plays in the world."

Only ~8% of the Vaca Muerta is under development, and the play is estimated to hold the world's second-largest shale gas and fourth-largest shale oil resources, according to U.S. government statistics.

data.title: Intuitive Surgical beats on both lines on da Vinci growth
data.description:
• Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) is up ~2% in after-hours trading Thursday after posting Q4 results that beat on both lines driven by strong year-over-year growth in placements of its da Vinci surgical systems.
  • In the quarter ended Dec. 31, the company placed 532 units compared to 493 in the year-ago period.
  • Intuitive was helped in the quarter by a 19% YoY increase in revenue to $2.87B.
  • Q4 non-GAAP EPS of $2.53 compares to $2.21 in Q4 2024.
  • Intuitive ended 2025 with cash, cash equivalents, and investments of ~$9B, compared to $8.8B at the end of 2024. 
  • In 2026, the company sees da Vinci procedure growth of ~13%-15% compared to 18% in 2025.

data.title: Aris Mining streamlines board and management, appoints founder as CEO
data.description:
• Ian Telfer retired as chair and director of Aris Mining (ARIS:CA) but will remain an advisor.
  • Founder Neil Woodyer was appointed chair and chief executive officer.
  • David Garofalo was named lead independent director to strengthen independent board oversight.
  • Doug Bowlby was promoted to president.
  • The chief operating officer role was eliminated, and Richard Thomas has departed the company.

data.title: Gold and silver rack up new record highs on geopolitical tensions, softer U.S. dollar
data.description:
Gold futures closed above $4,900/oz for the first time Thursday, and silver also surged to a fresh all-time high, sparked by ‌continued geopolitical tensions, a softer U.S. dollar and expectations of rate cuts from the U.S. Federal Reserve.

The gains came even after President Trump said a "framework of a future deal" for U.S. access to Greenland was reached and that new tariffs against European Union countries would not be implemented, as the details of any agreement were unclear.

The latest report on U.S. Personal Consumption Expenditures showed rising consumer spending in November and October, indicating a third straight quarter of strong growth.

Goldman Sachs raised its year-end gold price forecast to $5,400/oz from $4,900/oz previously, citing a broadening of private sector diversification into bullion.

"We assume that private sector diversification buyers - whose purchases hedge global policy risks and have driven the upside surprise to our price forecast - don't liquidate their gold holdings in 2026, effectively lifting the starting point of our price forecast," Goldman wrote, adding that it expects central bank purchases to average 60 tons in 2026 as emerging market central banks continue diversifying reserves into gold, contributing to the price increase.

Silver also is continuing its scorching rally, establishing a new record high above $95/oz, and has a "far more compelling fundamental narrative than gold," Tradu senior analyst Nikos Tzabouras said in a note. "Maybe it's not a reserve asset in the way that gold is, but it still benefits from safe-haven flows, and ​dollar weakness."

Although the silver market remains well supported over the long term, BCA Research chief strategist Roukaya Ibrahim warned a speculative frenzy has become the main driving force behind the metal's rally and could prove unsustainable.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery closed +1.6% to $4,908.80/oz, and front-month January Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled +4.1% to $95.976/oz, both new record highs.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Streamex plans proposed public offering
data.description:
• Streamex (NASDAQ: STEX) announced on Thursday that it intends to offer shares of common stock in an underwritten public offering, without disclosing the size or terms of the sale.
  • The company plans to use the net proceeds to repay prior indebtedness and for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • Needham & Company and Siebert are acting as joint book-running managers of the offering.
  • Shares +12.34%.

data.title: Legato Merger prices $200M initial public offering
data.description:
• Legato Merger (LEGO.U) on Thursday, said that it has priced its initial public offering of 20M units at $10 per unit, raising $200M, expected to begin trading on Jan. 23, 2026, and underwriters are granted a 45-day option to purchase up to 3M additional units at the IPO price.
  • Each unit consists of one ordinary share and one-third of a redeemable warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable to purchase one ordinary share at $11.50.
  • BTIG, LLC is acting as the sole book-running manager for the offering.

data.title: Albertsons prices $2.1B senior notes offering
data.description:
• Albertsons Companies (NYSE: ACI) on Thursday said that it has priced an upsized private offering of $1.2 billion of 5.625% senior notes due 2032 and $900 million of additional 5.750% senior notes due 2034.

  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 2, 2026.

  • Proceeds, together with cash on hand, will be used to redeem $1.35 billion of 4.625% senior notes due 2027 and $750 million of 5.875% senior notes due 2028, as well as to pay related fees and expenses.

  • The additional 2034 notes will be issued under the same indenture as Albertsons’ existing 5.750% senior notes due 2034.
  • ACI +0.11% after hours to $17.2.
  • Source: Press Release
